_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize statewide-exec batch: MI LG Gilchrist (D), MO AG Hanaway (R), MO LG Wasinger (R), MS LG Hosemann (R), MS AG Fitch (R). Evidence-first; every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence. CURRENCY FIXES: Gilchrist dropped out of the 2026 MI gov race (Jan 2026) and is now the Democratic nominee for MI Secretary of State (won convention Apr 19 2026, general Nov 3 2026 vs R Anthony Forlini) -> general_candidate. Hanaway is the SITTING appointed MO AG (Gov Kehoe appointed Aug 2025, sworn Sep 8 2025 after Bailey left for the FBI), NOT a 2026 candidate -> incumbent. HIGH-VALUE DEVIATIONS (cross-party): Hosemann (R) champions Medicaid expansion (refuse_federal_overreach q1 FALSE) and opposes private-school vouchers/ESAs (family q0 FALSE) -> pulls his score below party-default.

garlin-gilchrist.notes_set: MI Lieutenant Governor (Whitmer admin) since Jan 2019; term-limited as LG (term ends Jan 2027). CANDIDACY CURRENCY: entered the 2026 MI governor's race Mar 11 2025, then DROPPED OUT Jan 2026 ('no clear path'); is now the Democratic nominee for MICHIGAN SECRETARY OF STATE -- won the party convention on the first ballot Apr 19 2026 (MI nominates SoS by convention); general election Nov 3 2026 vs. Republican Anthony Forlini. (Notable: seeking the office that administers Michigan elections.) Consistent progressive across every axis with documented evidence; NO conservative deviation found. Pro-abortion (Reproductive Freedom for All / NARAL-successor endorsement; touts passing Prop 3 2022). LGBTQ ally (HRC PAC-endorsed; proclaimed Transgender Empowerment Month Oct 2025). Opposed the Secure MI Vote photo-ID/drop-box initiative ('anti-democracy and anti-American'). Championed MI's red-flag, safe-storage, and expanded-background-check gun laws. Opposed the Romulus ICE detention center (2026). Self-identifies as a gun owner but advocates restrictions (not a deviation). Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: Endorsed by Reproductive Freedom for All (NARAL successor) as a 'proven reproductive freedom champion'; touts passing Prop 3. Pro-abortion-rights; does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Champions abortion access (Prop 3 2022 constitutional amendment) -> opposite of abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Reproductive Freedom for All (NARAL successor), an abortion-rights advocacy organization.

0.note: HRC PAC-endorsed 'strong and effective ally'; LGBTQ champion. Does not affirm marriage as man-woman only.

1.note: Champion of the Elliott-Larsen LGBTQ civil-rights expansion; supports LGBTQ legal equality.

2.note: Proclaimed Transgender Empowerment Month (Oct 2025); 'won't be apologetic' protecting trans Michiganders -> does not affirm immutable biological sex.

4.note: Active LGBTQ/transgender promotion in public policy.

1.note: Called the Secure MI Vote initiative (photo voter ID + absentee-application restrictions) 'anti-democracy and anti-American' -> contrary to photo voter ID with citizenship verification.

3.note: Opposed Secure MI Vote drop-box hour limits and absentee restrictions; defends expansive mail/absentee voting -> contrary to opposing mass mail-in/drop boxes.

1.note: Joined/spoke at rallies opposing the Romulus ICE detention center (2026); pro-migrant -> contrary to mandatory deportation.

0.note: Championed MI's red-flag, safe-storage, and expanded-background-check laws -> opposite of permitless constitutional carry.

1.note: Championed the MI red-flag (extreme-risk) law (called it 'a floor') and expanded background checks.

catherine-hanaway.notes_set: Sitting Attorney General of Missouri (45th; first woman MO AG) -- Gov. Kehoe APPOINTED her Aug 19 2025; sworn in Sep 8 2025 after Andrew Bailey resigned to become FBI Co-Deputy Director. Term runs to Jan 8 2029; she has said she intends to run for a full term in 2028 (there is NO 2026 MO AG race -- the prior 'Attorney General candidate' framing was stale). Deep prior record: first woman MO House Speaker (2003-2005), U.S. Attorney E.D. Missouri (2005-2009, 4,000+ cases). Uniformly conservative across all 10 axes with no deviations. Pro-life (defended MO parental-consent law -> Missouri Right to Life thanked her by name; challenging Amendment 3; mifepristone suits). Praised MO Supreme Court upholding the SAFE Act (gender-transition ban for minors). Backed SJR 39 religious-liberty amendment; opposed MONA. Defended MO Voter ID law; led multistate election-integrity coalitions. Career prosecutor; removed a corrupt St. Louis sheriff. First-in-the-nation suit to exclude illegal aliens from the census. Passed MO's first concealed-carry law as Speaker (NRA 'True Ally'). Multistate suits vs. EPA, census, federal abortion rules. Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: Long pro-life record; as AG defended MO parental-consent abortion law (Missouri Right to Life publicly thanked her) and is challenging Amendment 3 statute-by-statute to preserve restrictions.

4.note: Pro-life; Missouri Right to Life-aligned. No abortion-industry (PP/NARAL/EMILY's List) funding.

0.note: Backed SJR 39 (religious-liberty amendment re gay-marriage participation); 1998 'no same-sex marriage.' Affirms traditional marriage.

1.note: Opposed the Missouri Nondiscrimination Act (MONA, LGBT employment protections); backed SJR 39 -> opposes same-sex-marriage legal equality.

2.note: Praised MO Supreme Court upholding the SAFE Act (bans gender-transition procedures for minors): 'landmark victory for Missouri families' -> affirms biological sex.

4.note: Supports SAFE Act and opposed MONA -> opposes LGBTQ promotion in policy/schools.

0.note: Documented support for state-funded vouchers for public & private schools (school choice).

1.note: Defended parental-consent law and the SAFE Act (no gender interventions for minors) -> supports parental consent on minors' medical/gender care.

Backed SJR 39 conscience protections; 'religious freedom rights are individual freedoms... our Bill of Rights places religious freedom first'; cited Trinity Lutheran -> supports conscience exemptions.

As AG defended MO Voter ID law before the MO Supreme Court (Nov 2025) and led a multistate coalition defending a voter-registration citizenship-verification order -> supports photo voter ID + citizenship verification.

0.note: Career prosecutor (U.S. Attorney E.D. MO, 4,000+ cases); as AG secured court-ordered removal of a corrupt St. Louis sheriff -> affirmative pro-law-enforcement record.

2.note: Record of proportional punishment: 'no parole for repeat felons,' supports death penalty; extensive criminal-prosecution career.

1.note: As AG, multistate suits against federal action (census, EPA grant 'misconduct', federal abortion-drug rules) -> declines/contests unconstitutional federal action.

2.note: Resisted federal overreach on life (abortion-drug rules) and defended state authority on gender-care/education -> resists federal overreach on life/education.

3.note: Consistent state-sovereignty litigation posture against federal action.

4.note: First-in-the-nation suit vs. Commerce/Census Bureau over counting illegal aliens for apportionment -> opposes federal capture of state representation/data.

2.note: Census suit argues sanctuary states 'steal' seats/electoral votes/funding via illegal-alien counts -> opposes sanctuary-state policy.

0.note: Passed MO's first concealed-carry law as House Speaker (overrode the governor's veto); backs constitutional carry; NRA 'True Ally.'

1.note: NRA-endorsed 'A'-rated 'True Ally'; championed gun-manufacturer liability protection -> opposes gun-control restrictions.

dave-wasinger.notes_set: Sitting Lieutenant Governor of Missouri (49th) -- elected Nov 5 2024 (def. Democrat Richard Brown 57-38), sworn in Jan 13 2025. Former St. Louis attorney/CPA; first elected office (LG casts no policy votes), so the record is THIN -- scored only on cited campaign positions, endorsements, and official actions; everything unsupported left null. Roman Catholic. Pro-life: exclusive Missouri Right to Life PAC endorsement; 'valuing life from conception to natural death is a core principle'; opposed Amendment 3. Hardline immigration: campaign ad vowed to deport illegal immigrants ('you come here legally or it's adios amigos'), backed sending MO troops to the border. No documented non-conservative deviation; record is thin, not heterodox. (NRA 'Kirk Fordice Freedom Award' appeared in an NLGA bio book but could not be independently confirmed -> guns left null. No same-sex-marriage, election-integrity, or family-policy statements found.) Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: Exclusive Missouri Right to Life PAC endorsement; 'valuing life from conception to natural death is a core principle of my campaign' -> affirms life from conception.

4.note: Missouri Right to Life-endorsed pro-life candidate; no abortion-industry (PP/NARAL/EMILY's List) funding.

0.note: Said Missouri should spend money to send troops to the U.S.-Mexico border -> supports active military border presence.

1.note: Campaign ad vowed to deport illegal immigrants: 'Illegals break our laws... You come here legally or it's adios amigos' -> supports mandatory deportation.

delbert-hosemann.notes_set: Lieutenant Governor of Mississippi (R) since Jan 2020, re-elected 2023 (~60% over Democrat Ryan Grover); presides over the 52-member MS Senate; TERM-LIMITED in 2027 (cannot seek a third LG term; term ends Jan 2028). Former MS Secretary of State 2008-2020. Establishment/moderate Republican, repeatedly attacked from the right -- the dossier surfaced THREE documented cross-party deviations: (1) HIGH-VALUE: leading MS Republican proponent of MEDICAID EXPANSION (announced a Senate expansion bill Feb 2024, backed it since 2019) -> refuse_federal_overreach q1 FALSE; (2) OPPOSES private-school vouchers/ESAs and killed school-choice bills in his Senate (WSJ, Empower MS, and the Center for Public Policy faulted him from the right) -> family q0 FALSE; (3) backed removing the Confederate emblem from the state flag (2020) and opposed full income-tax elimination (favored cautious phased cuts) -- noted but not cleanly scoreable. Remains conventionally conservative on life (NRTL-endorsed, 100% pro-life voting record), guns (NRA 'A'), voter ID (authored MS Initiative 27), and pro-police (Capitol Police expansion). Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: Post-Dobbs: 'I am pro-life. I am also pro-child.' National Right to Life endorsed him; MS Right to Life cited a '100% pro-life voting record.' Created a post-Dobbs Senate study group on women/children/families.

4.note: NRTL-endorsed, 100% pro-life voting record; no abortion-industry funding.

0.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: opposes private-school vouchers/ESAs and let school-choice/portability/charter/special-needs-ESA bills die in his Senate; WSJ and Empower MS faulted him from the right; the Center for Public Policy said his position 'aligns more with liberals.' -> contrary to universal school choice.

1.note: As Secretary of State, sponsored MS Initiative 27 (2011) creating voter ID, approved by 62% of voters -> supports photo voter ID.

0.note: Pushed the 2023 expansion of state-run Capitol Police in Jackson (officers ~105 -> 150) framed as crime reduction -> affirmative pro-law-enforcement action.
NRA-PVF 'A' rating, NRA member, NRA Kirk Fordice Freedom Award recipient; campaign pledge to defend 2A rights -> supports gun rights / constitutional carry posture (MS has permitless carry).

1.note: NRA 'A' rating -> opposes gun-control restrictions (red-flag/mag limits/AWB).

HIGH-VALUE CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: leading MS Republican proponent of Medicaid expansion (announced a Senate expansion bill Feb 2024 to 138% FPL; backed expansion since 2019) -> embraced the federal Obamacare program/strings, contrary to declining federal mandates.

lynn-fitch.notes_set: Attorney General of Mississippi (40th; first woman MS AG, first Republican MS AG since 1878) since Jan 2020, re-elected 2023 (~58% over Democrat Greta Kemp Martin); former MS State Treasurer 2012-2020. LANDMARK: brought/led Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization, the case that overturned Roe v. Wade (June 2022). Uniformly conservative across all 10 axes; no deviation found. Pro-life: Dobbs, EMTALA/Idaho (Moyle) amicus, mifepristone opposition. Title IX litigation vs. Biden gender-identity rule (multiple wins). Parental-rights amicus + defends MS social-media age-verification law (NetChoice v. Fitch) + pressed PornHub on minors. Led a 22-state Navy SEAL religious-liberty amicus; applauded the Coach Kennedy school-prayer decision. Anti-ESG (challenged ISS/Glass Lewis, BlackRock, UN-led bank alliance). Election integrity: Texas v. PA, Boockvar amicus, sued over federal voter-registration EO 14019. Pro-2A amici (Bruen, Snope/AR-15-ban, GOA v. Garland). Border: 287(g), supported TX border barriers, backed TdA-gang deportation. Multistate suits vs. Biden vaccine mandates. (Caution: 'Fitch Ratings' probe stories refer to the credit agency, not the AG.) Evidence-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: Brought/led Dobbs, defending MS's 15-week ban and asking the Court to overrule Roe/Casey -> strongest possible pro-life action.

1.note: Led the case that overturned Roe (enabling MS's near-total ban) and the EMTALA/Idaho amicus defending state abortion bans; opposes mail/telehealth abortion pills -> active abortion-abolition advocacy.

2.note: Litigated against the Biden Title IX rule expanding 'gender identity' (multiple wins: 'victory for women and girls'); SCOTUS sided with MS rejecting the rule -> affirms immutable biological sex.

4.note: Title IX litigation against gender-identity expansion in schools/sports -> opposes LGBTQ promotion in schools/policy.

1.note: Filed a parental-rights amicus (Nov 2023) and defends MS HB1126 (Social Media Safety Act, age verification + parental consent for minors) in NetChoice v. Fitch -> supports parental notification/consent for minors.

3.note: Pressed PornHub on a loophole endangering children and defends MS age-verification/parental-consent law for minors -> supports age-verification on pornographic content.

1.note: Led a 22-state amicus backing Navy SEALs' religious objections to the COVID vaccine mandate; VA religious-display case -> supports religious conscience exemptions.

3.note: Applauded the Coach Kennedy school-prayer decision and a VA religious-display case -> supports prayer/Christian symbols in the public square.

4.note: Anti-ESG: 21-state challenge to proxy firms ISS/Glass Lewis over ESG/net-zero voting; BlackRock letter; 19-state probe of banks in a UN-led ESG alliance -> opposes ESG capture.

1.note: Sued the Biden administration over the federal voter-registration order (EO 14019) and joined election-integrity coalitions -> supports state-controlled voter verification / photo ID posture.

3.note: Joined Texas v. Pennsylvania and the Boockvar amicus against PA's 3-day mail-ballot extension -> opposes mass mail-in expansion.

0.note: Joined a 27-state coalition supporting Texas border defense barriers -> supports physical border barrier.

1.note: Supported Trump's deportation of the Tren de Aragua gang; touted MS's ICE 287(g) partnership -> supports deportation enforcement.

2.note: ICE 287(g) partnership and suits against Biden immigration policy -> opposes sanctuary policy.

0.note: Joined the NYSRPA v. Bruen amicus supporting the right to carry outside the home -> supports carry rights.

1.note: Joined the 29-state Snope v. Brown amicus opposing Maryland's AR-15 ban; GOA v. Garland cert petition -> opposes 'assault-weapons' bans / gun control.
Filed multiple challenges to Biden COVID vaccine mandates (federal contractors, etc.) -> declined unconstitutional federal mandates.

2.note: Resisted federal overreach on life (abortion-drug rules, EMTALA), guns (2A amici), education/religious liberty (Title IX) -> resists federal overreach across rubric axes.

3.note: Consistent multistate state-sovereignty litigation posture against federal action.

4.note: Sued over the federal voter-registration EO 14019 -> opposes federal capture of state elections.
